title: Boat Hire in Lake Bled: Costs, Choices and 2026 Tips
html:
Lake Bled offers a sublime setting for boat hire, with departures from the town jetty near the castle. Expect prices from €40 for a small motorboat half-day to €400 for a crewed yacht. BoatHire24 connects you with verified listings across this Alpine lake.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Lake Bled?

Renting a boat in Lake Bled costs between €40 and €500 depending on the type and duration. Shared tours start at €15 per person, while private motorboat hire begins around €80 per hour. For a full-day catamaran experience, expect to pay €250 to €400. Crewed motor yachts, such as those listed through yacht charter Lake Bled, can go over €500 for a luxury half-day.

Where to set off in Lake Bled

The main departure point is the Bled Town Jetty, near the Park Hotel. Other real locations include Mlino, a small settlement with a slipway, the Sava River outlet at the eastern shore, and the Camping Bled dock. For longer trips, the nearby village of Ribno offers quieter moorings. Each spot gives easy access to Bled Island and the castle cliff.

Which boat to rent

  1. Rowboat or electric boat, ideal for first-time visitors and couples; can carry 2, 4 people; from €15 per hour.
  2. Motorboat (4, 5 metres), best for small groups wanting flexibility; up to 6 passengers; from €40 per half-day.
  3. Speedboat (6, 8 metres), for faster crossings to the island; accommodates 5, 8 guests; from €100 per hour.
  4. Catamaran sailing boat, perfect for day trips with family; sleeps 6, 8; from €250 per full day.
  5. Luxury motor yacht (10m+), includes models like the Fairline Targa 43 listing; carries 6+ guests with a skipper; from €400 per half-day.

Cost breakdown for boat hire

  • Shared tour to Bled Island: €15, €25 per person, approx. 50 minutes.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €40, €80, no licence required.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €250, €400, includes skipper.
  • Crewed yacht half-day: €350, €500, includes refreshments and swim stops.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Lake Bled?

For most small motorboats and electric boats under 15 hp no licence is required in Slovenia. Operators typically offer a brief safety briefing instead. For larger yachts or speedboats over 15 hp, a valid boating licence (such as an ICC or national equivalent) is normally required, but the operator will confirm the exact requirements when you book. The high season runs from June to September, when the prevailing breeze is the 'Bled burja', a light afternoon wind.

For a quieter experience, aim to launch just after 8am before the tour boats arrive; the water is glassy and the island reflections are at their best.

At a glance

  • Price band: €15, €500 per trip
  • Peak months: June, September 2026
  • Trip length: 50 minutes to 8 hours
  • Capacity: 1, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Lake Bled

Can I rent a boat in Lake Bled without a licence?

Yes, many small motorboats and rowboats are available without a licence, but you must attend a short safety briefing before departure.

How long does it take to reach Bled Island by boat?

The trip from the town jetty to Bled Island is about 1.5 nautical miles and takes roughly 10 to 15 minutes with a motorboat.

Are there boats for larger groups on Lake Bled?

Yes, catamarans and motor yachts can carry up to 12 passengers, making them ideal for family outings or celebrations.

Is it safe to swim from a rented boat in Lake Bled?

Swimming is allowed away from designated swimming zones, but always check with your boat operator about local rules and water safety.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Lake Bled or list your own. For owners, listing your vessel on boathire24.com is a simple way to reach verified renters planning their 2026 holidays.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Lake Bled and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
